mcp.name: biomate_session
mcp.title: Run BioMate Session
mcp.description:
**Primary entry point — use this for 90% of requests.** Run a complete BioMate scientific session from a natural-language goal. BioMate selects the right workflow from 2,455 indexed pipelines, pre-fills parameters from your goal text, executes on BioMate cloud, handles QC gates with auto-loop remediation, and produces structured findings. While running, the tool streams real-time progress (phase started, step completed, QC gate, auto-loop remediation, finding) back to the host. Returns a final run summary, a deep link to the live results panel, and the report URL. 

**How to write the `goal` parameter** — plain English, one to three sentences:
• Include the *what*: analysis type + subject (e.g. 'ADMET screening', 'RNA-seq DE', 'variant calling')
• Include *data location*: inline SMILES/sequences, S3 paths, accession numbers, or upload first with upload_file
• Include key *parameters* that matter: organism, library type, comparisons, thresholds
• You can omit anything BioMate can infer (it will ask if genuinely ambiguous)

**Good examples:**
  'Screen aspirin (CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O) and caffeine (Cn1cnc2c1c(=O)n(c(=O)n2C)C) for hERG inhibition, CYP3A4, and oral bioavailability'
  'RNA-seq differential expression on s3://lab-bucket/exp42/fastqs/ — human GRCh38, dUTP strand-specific, treated (n=3) vs control (n=3), FDR 0.05'
  'Whole-genome variant calling on the uploaded FASTQ pair, GRCh38, GATK HaplotypeCaller, germline mode'
  'Run homogeneous 3D refinement in CryoSPARC on s3://cryo/job042/, C2 symmetry, box size 256'
  'Fetch GSE183947 from GEO and run the same RNA-seq pipeline'

Use run_workflow instead when the user wants to call a specific workflow by ID with explicit parameter control.

goal.description: Natural-language scientific goal. Include: analysis type, data location (inline SMILES/sequences, s3:// paths, or GEO/SRA accession numbers), and key parameters (organism, comparisons, thresholds). BioMate infers the rest. Examples: 'Screen these 5 SMILES for hERG IC50 and CYP3A4 inhibition', 'RNA-seq DE on s3://bucket/exp1/ human GRCh38 paired-end treated vs control', 'Fetch GSE183947 and run differential expression'.

inputs.description: Optional structured inputs (S3 keys, sequences, SMILES, parameter overrides). BioMate merges these with what it extracts from `goal`. Example: {"smiles_list": ["CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O"], "organism": "human"}

experiment_id.description: Optional experiment to attach this run to (from recall_memory).

stream.description: Emit progress notifications during execution. Default true. Set false for hosts without MCP notification support — then poll with get_run.

mcp.name: search_workflow
mcp.title: Search Workflows
mcp.description: Search the BioMate workflow catalog (2,455 indexed workflows across 34 domains) by natural language. Returns ranked workflow cards with id, name, domain, one-line description, and estimated BioMate cloud cost. Use this when the user wants to pick a workflow explicitly; otherwise prefer biomate_session.

query.description: Natural language description of the analysis.

domain.description: Optional domain filter: transcriptomics, genomics, proteomics, drug_discovery, cryo_em, etc.

mcp.name: get_workflow_spec
mcp.title: Get Workflow Spec
mcp.description: Return the full specification for a workflow: required + optional parameters (with types and allowed values), default QC profile and thresholds, expected input files, estimated cost and runtime, and any license requirements. Call this before run_workflow when the user wants explicit parameter control.

mcp.name: run_workflow
mcp.title: Run Workflow
mcp.description: Execute a specific BioMate workflow on BioMate cloud with explicit parameters. Returns a run_id immediately. If stream=true, also emits progress notifications until the run terminates (same events as biomate_session). Use biomate_session instead when the user gave you a natural-language goal.

params.description: Parameter dict. Required params come from get_workflow_spec.

stream.description: Emit progress notifications while running. Default false.

mcp.name: get_run
mcp.title: Get Run Details
mcp.description: Return everything about a run in one call: status (pending|running|completed|failed), per-phase and per-step progress with timestamps, output files with download URLs, structured findings, QC gate results, and any auto-loop remediations applied. Replaces get_run_status + get_run_results + step-level findings polling.

mcp.name: cancel_run
mcp.title: Cancel Run
mcp.description: Cancel a running or queued BioMate workflow on BioMate cloud.

mcp.name: list_runs
mcp.title: List Runs
mcp.description: List the user's recent runs with status and timestamps. Filter by status or experiment.

mcp.name: preview_file
mcp.title: Preview Output File
mcp.description: Render a server-side preview of an output file (FASTA, VCF, CSV/TSV, image, PDF). Returns markdown + optional thumbnail PNG. Use this to show the user what an output looks like without downloading multi-GB files. For input QC of files the user is about to upload, prefer biomate_session (it runs a real QC workflow).

s3_key.description: The full s3_uri value (must start with 's3://') copied verbatim from a get_run output_files entry — e.g. 's3://bucket/path/file.json'. NOT a bare key; the s3:// prefix is required.

mcp.name: export_report
mcp.title: Export Report
mcp.description: Render a publication-ready report for a completed run as PDF or markdown. Includes the methods section, QC audit trail, structured findings, and figures. This is what users need for IND submissions, CRO compliance packages, and publication supplementary materials.

mcp.name: analyze_results
mcp.title: Analyze Results
mcp.description: Ask BioMate's AI to interpret a completed run. Returns natural-language analysis: key findings, quality assessment, scientific interpretation, recommended next steps. Use after get_run when the user asks 'what does this mean?'.

mcp.name: explain_error
mcp.title: Explain Run Error
mcp.description: Diagnose a failed run. Returns the likely root cause (genome mismatch, missing input, OOM, container pull failure, etc.) and the specific fix. Often the next step is run_workflow with corrected params.

mcp.name: query_database
mcp.title: Query Biological Database
mcp.description: Query a biological/chemical database by accession or name. Supported: uniprot, pdb, alphafold, ncbi_gene, dbsnp, clinvar, gnomad, kegg, reactome, chebi, chembl, hpo, string, pubmed.

query.description: Accession, gene symbol, or compound name.

mcp.name: resolve_accession
mcp.title: Resolve Accession
mcp.description: Identify a public archive accession (GEO, SRA, ENA, DDBJ) and return the best BioMate workflow to fetch it plus pre-filled params ready for run_workflow. Examples: GSE183947 → Geo Data Connector; SRR12345 → nf-core/fetchngs. Call this before run_workflow whenever the user provides an accession instead of a file.

accession.description: Public archive accession: GSE*, GSM*, GDS*, SRR*, SRX*, SRP*, ERR*, ERX*, ERP*, DRR*, PRJNA*, PRJEB*, E-MTAB-*, E-GEOD-*

mcp.name: browse_data
mcp.title: Browse Data Repository
mcp.description: Browse a biological data repository or S3 workspace by listing files and directories at a given path. Public sources (EBI, NCBI, Ensembl, UCSC) require a path. S3 sources (biomate_workspace, user_s3) accept an optional prefix. Use before fetch_public_data to navigate to the exact file you need.

source_id.description: Data source to browse. biomate_workspace = BioMate's S3 work bucket; user_s3 = user's own S3 bucket (if configured).

path.description: Directory path or S3 prefix to list (e.g. '/pub/databases/uniprot/' or 'results/run-xyz/').

mcp.name: fetch_public_data
mcp.title: Fetch Public Data
mcp.description: Download a file from a public biological repository (EBI, NCBI, Ensembl, UCSC) into BioMate's S3 workspace and return a presigned URL and S3 URI. Use the S3 URI as a workflow input parameter. Call browse_data first to find the exact path.

source_id.description: Data source the file comes from.

url.description: For http_public source: full HTTPS URL of the file to download.

mcp.name: recall_memory
mcp.title: Recall Memory
mcp.description: Retrieve relevant prior context for the current goal: past runs on similar inputs, validated procedures, findings tagged by the user, learned parameter preferences. Call before biomate_session for repeat users — it dramatically improves param auto-fill and avoids re-running work.

query.description: What to recall — usually the user's goal.

mcp.name: upload_file
mcp.title: Upload File
mcp.description: Get a one-shot signed S3 PUT URL so the host can upload a local file directly to BioMate's data plane without proxying bytes through the chat transport. Returns the s3_key to use in subsequent tool calls. For files >5MB this is mandatory; for small text payloads, inline strings are fine.

content_type.description: MIME type (auto-detected from filename if omitted).

function.description:
**Primary entry point — use this for 90% of requests.** Run a complete BioMate scientific session from a natural-language goal. While running, the tool streams real-time progress (phase started, step completed, QC gate, auto-loop remediation, finding) back to the host. Returns a final run summary, a deep link to the live results panel, and the report URL. 

**How to write the `goal` parameter** — plain English, one to three sentences:
• Include the *what*: analysis type + subject (e.g. 'ADMET screening', 'RNA-seq DE', 'variant calling')

• Include key *parameters* that matter: organism, library type, comparisons, thresholds

Returns the s3_key to use in subsequent tool calls. For files >5MB this is mandatory; for small text payloads, inline strings are fine.

content_type.description: MIME type (auto-detected from filename if omitted).

anthropic.name: biomate_session
anthropic.description:
**Primary entry point — use this for 90% of requests.** Run a complete BioMate scientific session from a natural-language goal. BioMate selects the right workflow from 2,455 indexed pipelines, pre-fills parameters from your goal text, executes on BioMate cloud, handles QC gates with auto-loop remediation, and produces structured findings. While running, the tool streams real-time progress (phase started, step completed, QC gate, auto-loop remediation, finding) back to the host. Returns a final run summary, a deep link to the live results panel, and the report URL. 

**How to write the `goal` parameter** — plain English, one to three sentences:
• Include the *what*: analysis type + subject (e.g. 'ADMET screening', 'RNA-seq DE', 'variant calling')
• Include *data location*: inline SMILES/sequences, S3 paths, accession numbers, or upload first with upload_file
• Include key *parameters* that matter: organism, library type, comparisons, thresholds
• You can omit anything BioMate can infer (it will ask if genuinely ambiguous)

**Good examples:**
  'Screen aspirin (CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O) and caffeine (Cn1cnc2c1c(=O)n(c(=O)n2C)C) for hERG inhibition, CYP3A4, and oral bioavailability'
  'RNA-seq differential expression on s3://lab-bucket/exp42/fastqs/ — human GRCh38, dUTP strand-specific, treated (n=3) vs control (n=3), FDR 0.05'
  'Whole-genome variant calling on the uploaded FASTQ pair, GRCh38, GATK HaplotypeCaller, germline mode'
  'Run homogeneous 3D refinement in CryoSPARC on s3://cryo/job042/, C2 symmetry, box size 256'
  'Fetch GSE183947 from GEO and run the same RNA-seq pipeline'

Use run_workflow instead when the user wants to call a specific workflow by ID with explicit parameter control.

goal.description: Natural-language scientific goal. Include: analysis type, data location (inline SMILES/sequences, s3:// paths, or GEO/SRA accession numbers), and key parameters (organism, comparisons, thresholds). BioMate infers the rest. Examples: 'Screen these 5 SMILES for hERG IC50 and CYP3A4 inhibition', 'RNA-seq DE on s3://bucket/exp1/ human GRCh38 paired-end treated vs control', 'Fetch GSE183947 and run differential expression'.

inputs.description: Optional structured inputs (S3 keys, sequences, SMILES, parameter overrides). BioMate merges these with what it extracts from `goal`. Example: {"smiles_list": ["CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O"], "organism": "human"}

experiment_id.description: Optional experiment to attach this run to (from recall_memory).

stream.description: Emit progress notifications during execution. Default true. Set false for hosts without MCP notification support — then poll with get_run.

anthropic.name: export_report
anthropic.description: Render a publication-ready report for a completed run as PDF or markdown. Includes the methods section, QC audit trail, structured findings, and figures. This is what users need for IND submissions, CRO compliance packages, and publication supplementary materials.

anthropic.name: upload_file
anthropic.description: Get a one-shot signed S3 PUT URL so the host can upload a local file directly to BioMate's data plane without proxying bytes through the chat transport. Returns the s3_key to use in subsequent tool calls. For files >5MB this is mandatory; for small text payloads, inline strings are fine.

content_type.description: MIME type (auto-detected from filename if omitted).
